description: Letter from Natan b. Avraham, in Fustat, to Abū Yūsuf Elʿazar b. Ismāʿīl, in Qūṣ. Dating: ca. 1035 CE (Gil). The address is probably identical with Elʿazar b Shemuel who signed T-S 13J1.8 in 1034 CE. The first section is a report on a member of the Ibn Sughmār family, who was suspected of having an affair with a Muslim woman or prostitute (אלזונה אלגויה). "He had already paid a fine of 120 gold pieces, an enormous sum, and had already been imprisoned for a month and a half at the writing of the letter reporting the scandal to someone in Qus, Upper Egypt; twelve court records had been written in this matter" (Med Soc V, p. 322, note 49). Abū Naṣr al-Tustarī involved himself somehow. The next part of the letter is a report on the prices of various mercantile goods. Also refers to a Jewish tax-farmer for tanning (hunā mutaqabbil al-dabāgha yahūdī). See Med. Soc., 2:605n21.
tags: prostitution, Natan b. Avraham, India, IB7, Qus
description_he: מכתב מנתן בן אברהם, פוסטאט, לאלעזר בן ישמעאל, קוס, כ-1035. על בן משפחת אבן סוגמאר שנחשד ביחסים עם אישה מוסלמית, שילם 120 דינר קנס ונכלא חודש וחצי. כן מדווח על מחירי סחורות.
